title: DeserializedUserConfig
description:
Per-user nextest configuration.

Stores personal preferences such as UI defaults and recording behavior.
This is distinct from the repository config (`.config/nextest.toml`),
which controls test execution.

See [_User configuration reference_](https://nexte.st/docs/user-config/reference)
for details on each setting.

experimental.description:
Toggles for experimental, non-stable features.

```toml
[experimental]
record = true
```

overrides.description:
Platform-specific overrides applied on top of the base configuration.

Each entry specifies a `platform` filter and any number of settings to
substitute when that filter matches. For each setting, the first
matching override wins; the base configuration is used if no override
matches.

record.description: Retention settings for the record-replay-rerun feature.

platform.description:
Target-spec expression selecting which platforms this override applies
to.

Accepts a target triple (e.g. `x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`) or a `cfg()`
expression (e.g. `cfg(windows)`, `cfg(target_os = "macos")`). Matched
against the platform nextest was built for.

record.description: Record retention settings to substitute on matching platforms.

ui.description: UI settings to substitute on matching platforms.

DeserializedRecordConfig.description:
Retention policy for recorded test runs.

Recording only happens when the `record` experimental feature is enabled
_and_ `enabled` here is true. The least-recently-used runs are evicted to
keep within `max-records`, `max-total-size`, and `max-age`.

enabled.description:
Whether to record test runs.

Set to false to temporarily disable recording without removing the rest
of the configuration. Has no effect unless the `record` experimental
feature is also enabled.

max-age.description: Maximum idle time before a recorded run is evicted (e.g. `"30d"`).

max-output-size.description:
Maximum size of a single captured stdout or stderr stream before it is
truncated (e.g. `"10MB"`).

max-records.description: Maximum number of recorded runs to retain.

max-total-size.description: Maximum combined size of all recorded runs (e.g. `"1GB"`).

DeserializedRecordOverrideData.description:
Per-platform substitutions for `[record]` settings.

Each field has the same meaning as in the `[record]` table. Only the
fields actually set here are substituted on matching platforms.

max-age.description: Maximum idle time before a recorded run is evicted (e.g. `"30d"`).

max-output-size.description:
Maximum size of a single captured stdout or stderr stream before it is
truncated (e.g. `"10MB"`).

max-records.description: Maximum number of recorded runs to retain.

max-total-size.description: Maximum combined size of all recorded runs (e.g. `"1GB"`).

DeserializedStreampagerConfig.description: Settings for nextest's built-in pager (active when `pager = ":builtin"`).

interface.description: How the pager uses the alternate screen and when it exits.

show-ruler.description: Shows a ruler at the bottom of the pager.

input-handler.description:
Enables the interactive keyboard input handler (e.g. `t` to dump test
status, `Enter` to print a summary line).

max-progress-running.description:
Maximum number of running tests to list in the progress bar.

Accepts a non-negative integer, or `"infinite"` for no limit. When the
number of running tests exceeds this, the remainder is collapsed into a
summary line.

output-indent.description: Indents captured test output for visual clarity.

pager.description:
Pager command to use for output that benefits from scrolling. Use
`":builtin"` to select nextest's built-in pager.

paginate.description: When to send output through the pager.

show-progress.description: Style of progress display shown during test runs.

streampager.description: Settings for the built-in pager (active when `pager = ":builtin"`).

DeserializedUiOverrideData.description:
Per-platform substitutions for `[ui]` settings.

Each field has the same meaning as in the `[ui]` table. Only the fields
actually set here are substituted on matching platforms.

input-handler.description: Enables the interactive keyboard input handler.

max-progress-running.description: Maximum number of running tests to list in the progress bar.

output-indent.description: Indents captured test output for visual clarity.

pager.description: Pager command to use for output that benefits from scrolling.

paginate.description: When to send output through the pager.

show-progress.description: Style of progress display shown during test runs.

streampager.description: Settings for the built-in pager (active when `pager = ":builtin"`).

ExperimentalConfig.description:
User-level experimental features.

Configured in the `[experimental]` table of the user config file:

```toml
[experimental]
record = true
```

Unknown features in this table produce a warning rather than an error, so
older nextest binaries can load configs written for newer ones.

record.description:
Enables the record-replay-rerun feature, which stores test run results
on disk so they can be replayed or selectively rerun later.

MaxProgressRunning.title: MaxProgressRunning
MaxProgressRunning.description: Maximum number of running tests to display: a non-negative integer, or "infinite" for no limit.

PaginateSetting.description: When to send output through the pager.

oneOf.description: Pages output from supported commands when stdout is a terminal.

StreampagerInterface.description: How the built-in pager uses the alternate screen and when it exits.

oneOf.description:
Exits immediately if the output fits on one page; otherwise switches
to full-screen and clears on exit.
Always uses full-screen mode and clears the screen on exit.

Waits briefly before entering full-screen mode; clears on exit only if
it switched to full-screen.

StreampagerWrapping.description: How long lines are wrapped in the built-in pager.

oneOf.description:
Disables wrapping; long lines extend off-screen and can be scrolled
horizontally.

UiShowProgress.description: Style of progress display shown during test runs.

oneOf.description:
Picks a display based on terminal capabilities: a progress bar in
interactive terminals, a counter otherwise.
Shows a progress bar listing the currently running tests.

Like `bar` in interactive terminals, but additionally hides successful
test output (sets `status-level` to `slow` and `final-status-level` to
`none`). Falls back to `auto` behavior in non-interactive contexts
(e.g. piped output, CI).
